---
title: "The Raph Round-up: April 2026"
author: "Raphael Eisenhofer"
date: "2026-05-11"
categories: [monthly]
image: "photos/fotm_thumbnail.jpg"
lightbox: true
---

I love April in Denmark. The spring ephemerals give way to trees, with fresh leaves giving the landscape a vibrancy. It was a great month for photography, and it took me quite a while to cull and process all the photos taken this month.

### Spring wildlife 1 (deer)

I also recently acquired a 600mm lens (the Nikon 600mm f/6.3 PF). I've always loved wildlife photography, so it's great to have some glass that can use for it. It's also quite light for its focal length, at only 1.5 kg! I went to the local deer park to try it out:

::: {layout-ncol="2"}
![Deer 1\
© 2026.](photos/deer1.jpg){group="deer"}

![Deer kiss. \
© 2026.](photos/deer2.jpg){group="deer"}
:::

::: {layout-ncol="1"}
![The rare three-headed deer.\
© 2026.](photos/deer3.jpg){group="deer"}
:::

::: {layout-ncol="3"}
![Deer 2\
© 2026.](photos/deer4.jpg){group="deer"}

![Western roe deer.\
© 2026.](photos/deer5.jpg){group="deer"}

![Herd.\
© 2026.](photos/deer6.jpg){group="deer"}
:::

### Spring wildlife 2 (birds)

With the trees waking -- and insect numbers increasing, bird activity also rises in April:

::: {layout-ncol="1"}
![Goldcrest (*regulus regulus*)\
© 2026.](photos/bird1.jpg){group="bird"}
:::

::: {layout-ncol="2"}
![Great spotted woodpecker peeking.\
© 2026.](photos/bird3.jpg){group="bird"}

![Common chaffinch.\
© 2026.](photos/bird2.jpg){group="bird"}
:::

::: {layout-ncol="2"}
![Eurasian treecreeper.\
© 2026.](photos/bird5.jpg){group="bird"}

![Great spotted woodpecker.\
© 2026.](photos/bird4.jpg){group="bird"}
:::

::: {layout-ncol="2"}
![Eurasian jackdaw.\
© 2026.](photos/bird6.jpg){group="bird"}

![Eurasian blue tit.\
© 2026.](photos/bird7.jpg){group="bird"}
:::

### Fresh spring leaves

For about a week or two, there is a real vibrancy to fresh tree leaves. Most people rave about autumn leaves, but I really love spring leaves -- especially when backlit by the sun.

::: {layout-ncol="3"}
![Leaves out, dogs out.\
© 2026.](photos/leaves1.jpg){group="leaves"}

![Silhouette.\
© 2026.](photos/leaves2.jpg){group="leaves"}

![Vibrant spring leaves 1.\
© 2026.](photos/leaves3.jpg){group="leaves"}
:::

::: {layout-ncol="1"}
![Vibrant spring leaves 2.\
© 2026.](photos/leaves4.jpg){group="leaves"}

![Vibrant birch.\
© 2026.](photos/leaves5.jpg){group="leaves"}
:::

::: {layout-ncol="3"}
![Vibrant park.\
© 2026.](photos/leaves6.jpg){group="leaves"}

![Three gnarly trees.\
© 2026.](photos/leaves7.jpg){group="leaves"}

![Fresh leaves.\
© 2026.](photos/leaves8.jpg){group="leaves"}
:::

### Local architecture

With longer and brighter evenings, I've also enjoyed getting out and taking photos of the local architecture.

::: {layout-ncol="3"}
![Siloen, Islandsbrygge.\
© 2026.](photos/architecture1.jpg){group="architecture"}

![Siloen, from the inside 1.\
© 2026.](photos/architecture2.jpg){group="architecture"}

![Siloen, from the inside 2.\
© 2026.](photos/architecture3.jpg){group="architecture"}
:::

::: {layout-ncol="5"}
![Sunset cacti 1.\
© 2026.](photos/architecture4.jpg){group="architecture"}

![Sunset cacti 2.\
© 2026.](photos/architecture5.jpg){group="architecture"}

![Sunset cacti 3.\
© 2026.](photos/architecture6.jpg){group="architecture"}

![Sunset cacti 4.\
© 2026.](photos/architecture7.jpg){group="architecture"}

![Sunset cacti 5.\
© 2026.](photos/architecture8.jpg){group="architecture"}
:::

::: {layout-ncol="1"}
![Eyes.\
© 2026.](photos/architecture9.jpg){group="architechture"}
:::

### Photo of the month

In the local park. I love the vibrancy of the fresh birch leaves, and the geometry of the brick walls.

![Fresh birch leaves.\
© 2026.](photos/fotm.jpg)

### Interesting things I've read/watched

Absolutely excellent resource about 'AI': [Modern-day Oracles or Bullshit machines](https://thebullshitmachines.com/index.html)? Can't recommend this enough.

Couple of great posts by Cory: [(1)](https://pluralistic.net/2026/04/08/process-knowledge-vs-bosses/) & [(2)](https://pluralistic.net/2026/04/12/always-great/).

[Great article](https://smallthingsconsidered.blog/schaechter/2026/04/nitrogen-obsession/) about nitrogen, and how biology figured out how to get it from the air. Also an [excellent journal article](https://enviromicro-journals.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/1751-7915.14159#mbt214159-bib-0075) about nitrogen.

[Right to repair is important](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NG-lLt5X3Rs)

[Video about AI slop](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xcq5XYkFJfY). Great [write-up](https://www.bloodinthemachine.com/p/why-the-ai-backlash-has-turned-violent) by Brian about AI-backlash.

[Factory farming](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9BYaVCc76bk) 👀. Also [related article](https://ourworldindata.org/most-people-care-about-farm-animals-our-food-system-doesnt-reflect-that?utm_source=OWID+Newsletter&utm_campaign=c98863ad47-owid-brief-2026-04-24&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-0c7f305164-537179702) about people's perception of how we treat animals vs. the food system that we have in place. [Research](https://www.scimex.org/newsfeed/top-meat-and-dairy-companies-greenwash-their-image-scientists-find) shows that dairy and meat companies greenwash – surprise surprise.

If you need some catharsis – [one of my favourite RATM songs](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kl4wkIPiTcY) - lyrics are 🔥.
